title: senecio aureus var. subnudus
intro: Senecio aureus var. subnudus, also known as golden ragwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of golden ragwort include its ability to protect the liver from damage, reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. Golden ragwort can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is often used in herbal medicine to support liver health and treat various digestive issues.
